questions.question: Derive the logistic regression model and explain how the sigmoid function is used.
keyPoints: Start with linear combination z = b0 + b1X1 + ... + bnXn
Models log-odds as linear function of features

questions.question: Why is log-loss used instead of MSE for logistic regression?
keyPoints: MSE with sigmoid creates non-convex surface
Log-loss penalizes confident wrong predictions more
MLE and log-loss minimization are equivalent

questions.question: Explain how coefficients in logistic regression are interpreted in terms of odds ratios.
keyPoints: Coefficient bi represents change in log-odds
e^bi gives the odds ratio for a unit change in Xi
Positive bi increases probability of positive class
Interpretation requires holding other variables constant

questions.question: Compare binary and multinomial logistic regression.
keyPoints: Binary: two classes, uses sigmoid function
Multinomial: more than two classes, uses softmax
Softmax normalizes outputs to sum to 1
Each class has its own set of coefficients
